searchDocs.title: Policy‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> type Policy = "AreAdsAllowed"¬†|¬†"ArePaidRandomItemsRestricted"¬†|¬†"AllowedExternalLinkReferences"¬†|¬†"IsContentSharingAllowed"¬†|¬†"IsEligibleToPurchaseCommerceProduct"¬†|¬†"IsEligibleToPurchaseSubscription"¬†|¬†"IsPaidItemTradingAllowed"¬†|¬†"IsPhotoToAvatarAllowed"¬†|¬†"IsSubjectToChinaPolicies" A string which represents policies returned by PolicyService.GetPolicyInfoForPlayerAsync.

searchDocs.title: all‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> QueryAuth.all( conditions:¬†{AuthCondition}-- List of conditions to evaluate. ) ‚Üí¬†AuthCondition-- A lambda that evaluates to see if ALL conditions are true. Combines multiple AuthConditions and only passes if all conditions are true.

searchDocs.title: any‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> QueryAuth.any( conditions:¬†{AuthCondition}-- List of conditions to evaluate. ) ‚Üí¬†AuthCondition-- A lambda that evaluates to see if ANY conditions are true. Combines multiple AuthConditions and only passes if any condition is true.

searchDocs.title: has‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> QueryAuth.has( amount:¬†number,-- The amount of conditions that must pass. conditions:¬†{AuthCondition}-- List of conditions to evaluate. ) ‚Üí¬†AuthCondition-- A lambda that evaluates to see if the amount of conditions are true. Combines multiple AuthConditions and only passes if amount conditions are true.

searchDocs.title: negate‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> QueryAuth.negate( condition:¬†AuthCondition-- The condition to negate. ) ‚Üí¬†AuthCondition-- A lambda that negates the provided condition. Returns true if the AuthCondition is false.

searchDocs.title: Policy‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> QueryAuth.Policy(policy:¬†Policy-Thepolicytocheck.) ‚Üí¬†AuthCondition-AlambdathatreturnsthevaluePolicyService provides. Checks if PolicyService allows the specified policy for the player.

searchDocs.title: Once‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> QueryAuth.Once() ‚Üí¬†AuthCondition-- A lambda that returns true if the player did not get checked by the condition before. Checks to see if the player has not met this condition before.

searchDocs.title: UserId‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> QueryAuth.UserId( values:¬†{number}?-- List of UserIds to check against. If nil, no UserId will pass. ) ‚Üí¬†AuthCondition-- A lambda that evaluates to see if the player's UserId is in the list. Checks if a player's UserId is in the provided list of UserIds.

searchDocs.title: AccountAge‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> QueryAuth.AccountAge( minimum:¬†number,-- Minimum account age in days. maximum:¬†number?-- Maximum account age in days. If nil, no maximum is enforced. ) ‚Üí¬†AuthCondition-- A lambda that evaluates to see if the player's account age meets the criteria. Checks if a player's account age meets the specified minimum and maximum days.

searchDocs.title: Team‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> QueryAuth.Team( values:¬†{string}?-- List of team names to check against. If nil, any team will pass. ) ‚Üí¬†AuthCondition-- A lambda that evaluates to see if the player's team name is in the list. Checks if a player's team name is in the provided list of team names.

searchDocs.title: Gamepass‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> QueryAuth.Gamepass( gamepass:¬†number-- The gamepass asset ID to check against. ) ‚Üí¬†AuthCondition-- A lambda that evaluates to see if the player owns the specified gamepass. Checks if the player owns the gamepass with the given asset ID.

searchDocs.title: Premium‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> QueryAuth.Premium() ‚Üí¬†AuthCondition-- A lambda that returns true if the player has premium. Checks to see if the player has premium.

searchDocs.title: ServerTime‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> QueryAuth.ServerTime(time:¬†number-Thetimetocheckagainst,inseconds.) ‚Üí¬†AuthCondition-Alambdathatreturnstrueiftheserver'stimeisgreaterorequaltotheprovidedtime. Checks to see if the server's time is greater or equal to the provided time.

searchDocs.title: Throttle‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> QueryAuth.Throttle( time:¬†number,-- The time to check against, time unit depends on which function was passed on mark, but by default it's in seconds. mark:¬†function-- An optional function which will be used to track intervals inside the function, defaults to os.clock ) ‚Üí¬†AuthCondition-- A lambda that returns true if passed time parameter is greater or equal to time passed since last truthy return. Checks to see if time (t) is greater or equal to time passed since the last time the condition returned true. This take into account intervals for individual players, tracking each interval for each player that was passed into the condition. For a global functionality that does not separate player's intervals, use QueryAuth.GlobalThrottle

searchDocs.title: Studio‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> QueryAuth.Studio() ‚Üí¬†AuthCondition-- A lambda that returns true if game is running in studio. Checks to see if the game is running in studio.

searchDocs.title: Badge‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> QueryAuth.Badge( badgeId:¬†number-- The badge ID to check against. ) ‚Üí¬†AuthCondition-- A lambda that evaluates to see if the player owns the specified badge. Checks if the player owns the badge with the given badge ID.

searchDocs.title: GlobalThrottle‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> QueryAuth.GlobalThrottle( time:¬†number,-- The time to check against, time unit depends on which function was passed on mark, but by default it's in seconds. mark:¬†function-- An optional function which will be used to track intervals inside the function, defaults to os.clock ) ‚Üí¬†AuthCondition-- A lambda that returns true if passed time parameter is greater or equal to time passed since last truthy return. Checks to see if time (t) is greater or equal to time passed since the last time the condition returned true. This does not account intervals for individual players, rather globally. For that functionality, use QueryAuth.Throttle

searchDocs.title: Group‚Äč
searchDocs.content: </> QueryAuth.Group( group_id:¬†number,-- The Roblox group ID to check against. min_rank:¬†number?¬†|¬†string?,-- The minimum rank the player must have in the group. max_rank:¬†number?¬†|¬†string?-- The maximum rank the player can have in the group. ) ‚Üí¬†AuthCondition-- A lambda that evaluates to see if the player is in the specified group with the given rank range. Checks if the player is in the specified group with an optional rank range.
